item.action: Detect material drift between privacy-safe OIDC temporal calibration cohorts before any team reuses prior calibration evidence.

progress.finding: Cohort calibration is observational and can become stale. Material skew growth, lifetime contraction, or not-before claim-shape change must route to review and must never automatically widen verifier policy.
progress.verification: 5 deterministic Node tests passed in a local runtime using byte-identical pre-commit files.
progress.uncertainty: Default drift thresholds and cohort minimums are engineering safeguards, not provider guarantees or universal statistical bounds.

item.handoff: Cloudflare Research should run this detector on independently verified R-009 summaries before policy review. Any drift_review_required result blocks reuse; no result authorizes token acceptance, verifier widening, deployment, diagnosis, or treatment claims.

reason: Shared queue state is maintained through additive projections to avoid overwriting concurrent team work.
